title: rlevante_walkthrough
text: Permission access data granted via Redivis, data-sharing platform used LEVANTE datasets. Public releases LEVANTE datasets Redivis include least four tables: participants, scores, surveys, trials. Table 1. Redivis tables commonly read data frames rlevante

what: Using rlevante to load LEVANTE data
title: rlevante_walkthrough
text: table Redivis can read R data frame using specific rlevante “get” function. “get” function takes least two arguments, including dataset name (required) version number (defaults current version unless otherwise specified). First, load rlevante packages, needed. , use get_participants obtain child participant ids, approximate birthdates, associated caregiver teacher ids. Use get_scores obtain scored cognitive task data.  Use get_trials access trial-level data. Use get_surveys access item-level survey data. Finally, researchers accessing LEVANTE data can use get_raw_data access additional tables within private datasets.
code: library(rlevante) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(ggplot2) theme_set(theme_classic()) participants <- get_participants("levante-data-example:d0rt", version = "current") #> Fetching data for levante-data-example:d0rt #> --Fetching table participants  participants |> count(dataset, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   dataset                  n #>   <chr>                <int> #> 1 pilot_mpieva_de_main    15 scores <- get_scores(data_source = "levante-data-example:d0rt") #> Fetching data for levante-data-example:d0rt #> --Fetching table scores  scores |> count(dataset, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   dataset                  n #>   <chr>                <int> #> 1 pilot_mpieva_de_main    33  scores |> count(task_id, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 11 × 2 #>    task_id                      n #>    <chr>                    <int> #>  1 vocab                        5 #>  2 hearts-and-flowers           4 #>  3 trog                         4 #>  4 egma-math                    3 #>  5 matrix-reasoning             3 #>  6 memory-game                  3 #>  7 mental-rotation              3 #>  8 same-different-selection     3 #>  9 theory-of-mind               3 #> 10 sre                          1 #> 11 swr                          1 ggplot(scores, aes(x = age, y = score)) +   facet_wrap(vars(task_id)) +   geom_point() trials <- get_trials("levante-data-example:d0rt") #> Fetching data for levante-data-example:d0rt #> --Fetching table trials  trials |> count(dataset, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   dataset                  n #>   <chr>                <int> #> 1 pilot_mpieva_de_main  1320  trials |> count(task_id, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 11 × 2 #>    task_id                      n #>    <chr>                    <int> #>  1 vocab                      242 #>  2 hearts-and-flowers         236 #>  3 trog                       153 #>  4 egma-math                  124 #>  5 swr                        120 #>  6 matrix-reasoning           106 #>  7 theory-of-mind             102 #>  8 same-different-selection    85 #>  9 mental-rotation             68 #> 10 memory-game                 52 #> 11 sre                         32 surveys <- get_surveys("levante-data-example:d0rt") #> Fetching data for levante-data-example:d0rt #> --Fetching table surveys  surveys |> count(dataset, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   dataset                  n #>   <chr>                <int> #> 1 pilot_mpieva_de_main    69 administrations <- get_raw_table(table_name = "administrations", data_source =  "levante-data-example-raw:bm7r") #> Fetching data for levante-data-example-raw:bm7r #> --Fetching table administrations  administrations |> count(public_name, sort = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 18 × 2 #>    public_name                     n #>    <chr>                       <int> #>  1 Paket 1                         2 #>  2 Paket 3                         2 #>  3 Group3_Roar                     1 #>  4 Group3_noR                      1 #>  5 Paket 2 Group 2                 1 #>  6 Paket 4 (kein Sprachtest)       1 #>  7 Paket 4 (mit Sprachtest)        1 #>  8 Paket 4 (mit Sprachtest) G2     1 #>  9 Paket2                          1 #> 10 Retest_G1&2_Roar                1 #> 11 Retest_G3                       1 #> 12 Retest_G3_R                     1 #> 13 Retest_Group1                   1 #> 14 Runde 1 (Nov 2025)              1 #> 15 Runde 2 (Januar 2026)           1 #> 16 Test_Group4_2                   1 #> 17 Test_Group4_TP1                 1 #> 18 Test_Survey_0825                1

title: Download LEVANTE Data
text: R package accessing LEVANTE data. useful links: - overview LEVANTE project. - Details LEVANTE’s scoring psychometrics. - Details LEVANTE child tasks. - access LEVANTE data. Prior completing steps described link, access example dataset toy data. - Data browser. can browse scored trial level task public data using data browser. partner access additional datasets Redivis, also able load browse datasets data browser.

title: Download LEVANTE Data
code: #install.packages(“devtools”) # if you need the devtools package, uncomment this line devtools::install_git(url="https://github.com/levante-framework/rlevante")

title: Download LEVANTE Data
text: collection “get” functions acquire LEVANTE data. example, use get_scores download scored cognitive task data.

title: Download LEVANTE Data
text: Permission access data granted via Redivis, data-sharing platform used LEVANTE datasets. Individuals seeking access LEVANTE data must create account Redivis sign data use agreement. Datasets available public include levante-data-pilots levante-example-data. partner access data private repository, see page information set data access. access dataset Redivis, can use rlevante read data directly R. Please see rlevante user walkthrough information examples.

title: Download LEVANTE Data
text: addition name, dataset reference ID. required, specifying ID stable code specifying dataset names, dataset names can easily edited. find use dataset’s reference ID, follow steps: Go dataset’s page Redivis. example, link publicly available pilot data release https://stanford.redivis.com/datasets/68kn-csrddrz5x. , click “API Information” right navigation panel.  pop-, check box upper right hand corner “Qualified references.”  Record string used specify dataset, including name (“levante_data_pilots”“) reference ID (“68kn”). rlevante functions, string can used argument identify dataset (e.g., get_participants(data_source = “levante_data_pilots:68kn”)).
